title: Creamy Irish Beef Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Irish dish featuring tender beef, rich praline-french mustard, and a hint of dashi extract, all while being diabetic-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the praline-french mustard and dashi extract.
Add the beef to the bowl and toss to coat with the mustard mixture.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and brown the beef on all sides.
Transfer the browned beef to a ramekin, leaving the drippings in the skillet.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, browned beef, salt, and pepper to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Cover the skillet and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2.5 hours or until the beef is tender.
Stir in the heavy cream and serve hot.
